Examination: Tooth 46 has a temporary crown in place, normal mucosa in the periapical area, no significant gingival swelling, and no percussion pain.

Medical Instructions: Advise the patient to avoid consuming excessively cold, hot, hard, or sticky foods. Return for follow-up if loosening or discomfort occurs, and schedule an appointment by phone before visiting.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Examination: Mandibular deviation to the left, maxillary posterior teeth misalignment, posterior open bite, uneven occlusal curve, right lower premolar nearing alignment, poor elastics cooperation.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Replaced upper archwire with 0.016*0.022 nickel-titanium wire, aligned posterior teeth, replaced lower power chain to continue space closure, and instructed on elastics wear.
Medical Instructions: Maintain oral hygiene, avoid hard or sticky foods, follow-up as needed.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105). Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Examination: General oral hygiene is average, with bleeding on probing, no periodontal pockets, presence of calculus (+), and staining (+). Gingiva is not swollen, and there is no attachment loss. Tooth 13 has a well-fitted implant restoration. Tooth 25 has a well-fitted crown restoration.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105).
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Treatment Recommendations: Tooth 16 shows a low-density shadow at the root base and widening of the periodontal ligament.
Management: The patient was informed of the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Full-mouth ultrasonic scaling, air polishing, irrigation, and polishing were performed, followed by iodine glycerin application.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Refrain from consuming pigmented beverages such as coffee, tea, and cola, and avoid smoking. Maintain oral hygiene and use mouthwash. Schedule regular follow-ups and return if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent tooth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Restoration.
Examination: Tooth 36 exhibits occlusal caries extending to the middle layer of dentin. Negative responses to probing, cold, heat, and percussion tests.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent tooth 36 (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: Restoration.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to the procedure. A rubber dam was placed, caries were removed, and the cavity was prepared. The cavity was cleaned and dried, and a calcium hydroxide liner was placed near the pulp. Acid etching was performed, followed by rinsing and drying. A bonding agent was applied, thinned, and light-cured. Composite resin was layered and light-cured, followed by shaping, occlusal adjustment, and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid chewing on the affected side for 2 hours. Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard objects.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Pulpitis (K04.005).
Examination: Teeth 45 and 47 are temporarily sealed.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis affecting teeth 45 and 47 (K04.005).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 47, the temporary crown and sealing material were removed, the abutment and restoration were cleaned, and the restoration was trial-fitted for marginal integrity and proximal contact. Occlusion was adjusted, polished, and permanently cemented. For tooth 45, the temporary seal was removed, and the tooth was restored with composite resin, adjusted, and polished. A crown restoration was recommended.
Medical Instructions: Maintain oral hygiene and avoid biting hard or sticky foods. Return for follow-up if any discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105). Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Examination: Teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 37, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47 show generalized gingival swelling and redness with easy bleeding upon probing. Presence of calculus and significant staining. Carious lesion on the occlusal surface of tooth 27.
Radiographic Examination: No significant findings.
Diagnosis: Gingivitis affecting teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 37, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47 (K05.105).
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival scaling for all teeth.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Intraoral photographs were taken. Full-mouth supragingival scaling, irrigation, air polishing, and iodine glycerin application were performed. Follow-up photographs were taken for comparison and oral hygiene education was provided.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperature stimuli for one week. Maintain oral hygiene with regular use of mouthwash. Schedule regular check-ups and return if any discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Dental caries (K02.901). Treatment: Filling.
Examination: Tooth 36 shows black discoloration at the buccal pit, not reaching the dentin layer. The probe catches slightly, but there is no percussion, probing, or cold sensitivity. Gingiva appears normal.
Radiographic Examination: No significant radiolucency observed on tooth 36.
Diagnosis: Dental caries (K02.901) on tooth 36.
Treatment Plan: Filling for tooth 36.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis were explained to the child and parents, who consented to the treatment. Caries removal was performed, cavity preparation was completed, caries gel was applied to soften the carious tissue, and decay was removed with instruments. The cavity was cleaned and dried, etched, rinsed, and dried again. A bonding agent was applied, thinned, and cured with light. Resin was layered and cured incrementally, shaped, occlusion adjusted, and polished.
Medical Instructions: Maintain oral hygiene, avoid hard or sticky foods, and return for follow-up if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Dental caries (K02.901).
Examination: Tooth 12 is temporarily sealed. The periapical mucosa is normal, and the tooth is non-tender to percussion.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Tooth 12: Dental caries (K02.901).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 12, the patient was informed about the advantages and disadvantages of different restorative materials, associated costs, treatment duration, potential follow-up visits, and complications. The patient opted for a fiber post and all-ceramic crown restoration. The temporary seal was removed, the post space was prepared, a fiber post was placed, resin filling was done, the abutment was prepared and refined, a silicone impression and bite registration were taken, and a temporary crown was placed. Shade matching was performed.
Medical Instructions: Follow-up if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard foods and maintain oral hygiene. Attend scheduled follow-up appointments.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105). Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Examination: Generalized gingival redness and swelling, bleeding on probing, no periodontal pockets. Heavy calculus (+++), staining (+++).
Radiographic Examination: No radiographs taken.
Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105).
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Informed the patient about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis. Performed air polishing, full-mouth supragingival scaling, and polishing. Applied fluoride varnish for caries prevention, used a steam eye mask, applied iodine glycerin, and provided oral hygiene education.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Avoid coffee, tea, cola, and smoking. Maintain oral hygiene and use mouthwash. Schedule regular check-ups and return if symptoms persist.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Resin restoration.
Examination: Poor oral hygiene with plaque accumulation at the cervical margins. Occlusal caries present, negative to probing, cold, and percussion tests. Gingiva appears normal.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries in tooth 46 (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: Resin restoration for tooth 46.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Applied caries gel to soften carious tissue, removed decay, prepared cavity, etched with 35% phosphoric acid, applied bonding agent, and restored with VOCO resin to recreate occlusal anatomy. Adjusted occlusion and polished. Provided oral hygiene instruction, including brushing and flossing techniques. Applied fluoride varnish after plaque disclosure and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Educate on oral hygiene, including brushing, flossing, and interdental brush use.
Remarks: Consider preventive or restorative treatment for teeth 17, 16, 26, 27, 37, and 47 at the next visit.

Examination: Maxillary central incisors aligned, overbite coverage 0.5mm, correction of crossbite and torsion. Mandibular lingual arch space maintainer is functioning normally.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 11 and 21 show symmetrical arches with open apices.
Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Conclude segmental arch correction for maxillary incisors. Maintain mandibular lingual arch.
Management: Tooth 11: Removal of fixed orthodontic appliances, adhesive removal, and polishing. Photographs and radiographs taken.
Medical Instructions: Follow-up if discomfort occurs. Schedule regular pediatric dental check-ups.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Examination: Mandibular deviation to the left, maxillary posterior teeth misalignment, posterior open bite, uneven occlusal curve, right lower premolar nearing alignment, poor elastics cooperation.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Replaced upper archwire with 0.016*0.022 nickel-titanium wire, aligned posterior teeth, replaced lower power chain to continue space closure, and instructed on elastics wear.
Medical Instructions: Maintain oral hygiene, avoid hard or sticky foods, follow-up as needed.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105). Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Examination: General oral hygiene is average, with bleeding on probing, no periodontal pockets, presence of calculus (+), and staining (+). Gingiva is not swollen, and there is no attachment loss. Tooth 13 has a well-fitted implant restoration. Tooth 25 has a well-fitted crown restoration.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105).
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Treatment Recommendations: Tooth 16 shows a low-density shadow at the root base and widening of the periodontal ligament.
Management: The patient was informed of the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Full-mouth ultrasonic scaling, air polishing, irrigation, and polishing were performed, followed by iodine glycerin application.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Refrain from consuming pigmented beverages such as coffee, tea, and cola, and avoid smoking. Maintain oral hygiene and use mouthwash. Schedule regular follow-ups and return if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: tooth structure defect (K07.303).
Examination: Tooth 46 retains a temporary crown with no soft tissue abnormalities.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Tooth structure defect in tooth 46 (K07.303).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 46, the patient was informed of the diagnosis, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. The temporary crown and sealing material were removed, the abutment and restoration were cleaned, and the restoration was tried in for fit and cemented with dual-cure resin cement, followed by occlusal adjustment and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid hard foods, maintain oral hygiene, and return for follow-up.
Remarks:
